next_pass.focus: Group velocity measurements in cortex + Topological neuroscience + Critical slowing down before insight

open_gaps: Group velocity measurements in cortex — Does anyone measure v_g = dω/dk in neural data? This would test whether v_g ≈ v_p or v_g < v_p.

Topological derivation of 2/3 in neural systems — If the dispersion relation is not ω ∝ k^{2/3}, why does the 2/3 ratio appear in sleep consolidation, memory assemblies, and Koide? Is there a neural topology that enforces it?
Phase gradient control mechanism — How does the DAN actually modulate phase gradients? Is this via oscillatory frequency, synaptic gain, or anatomical connectivity?
Individual differences in refractive index — If n ≈ 10^8 is fixed, what explains IQ differences? Better filtering algorithms? Faster reconfiguration (not higher velocity)?
Critical slowing down before insight — Does EEG show CSD signatures (increased variance, autocorrelation) before Aha moments? This would confirm the phase transition prediction.

passes.focus: Broad landscape survey — Wickens MRT, task switching, neural gating mechanisms
key_findings: Wickens MRT uses 'resource' language but predicts structural interference (compatible with impedance matching)
Task switch costs scale parametrically with rule dissimilarity — supports reconfiguration over interference
Oscillation-induced gating (Jahnke 2014) shows explicit impedance-like mechanism in neural tissue
Precision-weighting (predictive coding) is gain control — literally a gate mechanism
Gate model predicts dissimilar-task interference more than similar-task (Wickens data supports this)
Attention economy metaphor dominates public discourse but not cognitive neuroscience

passes.focus: Deepdive — Wickens original papers + neural oscillations as dispersion modulator + reconfiguration circuitry
key_findings: Attention is a dispersion modulator controlling the relationship between phase velocity (v_p) and group velocity (v_g)
Task-switching costs scale with neural distance (RSA dissimilarity) in the frontoparietal network (FPN)
IFJ identified as the 'hub' for gate reconfiguration; CSD (Critical Slowing Down) acts as the functional integration window during switches
Wickens' 4-D model dimensions map directly to propagation modes, depths, and independent media
Aria's SensorOrchestrator.kt is a functional prototype of the gate model, scaling causal velocity (intervalMs) by attention level

passes.focus: Synthesis — Refractive Mapping + Optimal Ratio Derivation (v_p vs v_g)
key_findings: Human cognitive refractive index (n) is approximately 10^9 informational bits/s reduction
Conscious throughput (v_cog) is strictly bounded at ~10 bits/s (hardware filter)
Top-down traveling waves in the DAN act as refractive lenses by modulating phase gradients (k)
The 2/3 ratio (T-010) is a topological identity derived from double-cover Moduli spaces (Bin Li, 2025)
Dispersion relation ω ∝ k^{2/3} implies v_g = 2/3 v_p (Optimal Information Flow)
The 2:1 (2/3) ratio is ubiquitous in memory reinforcement, assembly organization, and mRNA cycling

passes.focus: Verification — EEG Dispersion (2/3 Exponent) + Refractive Index vs. Expertise + DAN Phase Maps
key_findings: EEG dispersion relation is TRANSCENDENTAL (not power law) — v_g = 2/3 v_p NOT CONFIRMED, confidence dropped from 0.80 to 0.40
Conscious throughput ≈ 10 bits/s CONFIRMED across all tasks — expertise improves filtering, not capacity (Zheng & Meister 2024)
DAN traveling waves CONFIRMED — 6.5 m/s (alpha), anterior→posterior direction precedes perceptual switches
EEG power spectrum is 1/f (pink noise, β ≈ 1) — this describes spectral decay, NOT dispersion relation
Single neuron transmits ~10 bits/s — same as whole-brain throughput, suggesting serial access bottleneck
